description_long: This object is a firearm with a classic design, featuring a long, cylindrical barrel and a prominent wooden stock and foregrip. The receiver and barrel are constructed from dark metal, giving it a robust and utilitarian appearance. The wooden components have a polished, reddish-brown finish, contrasting with the matte black metal parts. The trigger and trigger guard are clearly visible, with the guard being rounded and proportionate to the grip. The magazine is a straight, box-type, inserted below the receiver. The overall form and details suggest a historical submachine gun style, commonly associated with early to mid-20th century urban environments.
description: A dark metal firearm with a polished wooden stock and foregrip, featuring a straight box magazine and a classic, robust design.
description_view_0: Narrow end-on view showing the slim profile of the barrel and foregrip, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the firearm, including the wooden stock, foregrip, and box magazine.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view, highlighting the alignment of the barrel and the top of the receiver.
description_view_3: Reverse side view showing the wooden stock, foregrip, and the other side of the receiver and magazine.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, medium length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with smooth edges and no finger grooves
